questions.question: Explain the three main stream classes used for file handling in C++ with their purposes.
keyPoints: ifstream - used for reading from files (input)
ofstream - used for writing to files (output)
fstream - used for both reading and writing operations

questions.question: List and explain any four file opening modes in C++.
keyPoints: ios::in - opens file for reading
ios::out - opens file for writing
ios::app - opens file for appending
ios::trunc - truncates existing file content
ios::binary - opens file in binary mode
ios::ate - opens and positions pointer at end

questions.question: How do you check whether a file was opened successfully in C++? Explain with example.
keyPoints: Use is_open() method - returns true if file is open
Use fail() method - returns true if operation failed
Example: if(!outfile) cout << "Error opening file";
Example: if(outfile.is_open()) cout << "File opened";

questions.question: What is the importance of closing a file after processing? Explain.
keyPoints: Flushes buffer - ensures all unwritten data is saved to file
Releases system resources - file handles are limited
Prevents data loss - unwritten data may be lost if not closed
Proper resource management - good programming practice

questions.question: Write a C++ program to open a file for writing and handle file opening errors.
keyPoints: Include <fstream> header
Create ofstream object
Check if file opened using is_open() or !fail()
Display error message if file not opened
Close file after operations

questions.question: Differentiate between ios::app and ios::ate file modes.
keyPoints: ios::app - appends data at end, maintains end position after each write
ios::ate - positions at end initially but allows seeking anywhere
ios::app is for sequential append only
ios::ate allows random access after opening at end

questions.question: Explain the default file opening modes for ifstream, ofstream, and fstream.
keyPoints: ifstream defaults to ios::in (read mode)
ofstream defaults to ios::out | ios::trunc (write with truncation)
fstream requires explicit ios::in and ios::out specification
ofstream creates file if it doesn't exist, ifstream doesn't

questions.question: Write a complete C++ program that opens a file named "student.txt", writes student information to it, closes the file, then opens it again to read and display the contents. Include proper error handling.
keyPoints: Include necessary headers
Create ofstream and open file
Check successful opening
Write data to file (name, roll, marks)
Close ofstream properly
Create ifstream and open same file
Read and display data using while loop with eof()
Close ifstream
Handle errors appropriately
Return 0 from main

questions.question: Explain the difference between text mode and binary mode file operations in C++. Write a program to demonstrate writing and reading data in binary mode.
keyPoints: Text mode - newline translation (\n to \r\n on Windows)
Binary mode - no translation, raw bytes
Binary mode uses ios::binary flag
Suitable for non-text data (images, structs)
Program should show write using write() method
Program should show read using read() method
Show proper opening with binary mode
Demonstrate sizeof() for binary data

questions.question: Create a C++ program that demonstrates the use of fstream class to perform both read and write operations on the same file. Use appropriate file modes.
keyPoints: Use fstream class for bidirectional I/O
Open with ios::in | ios::out mode
Write data to file first
Clear EOF flag and seek to beginning
Read and display the written data
Show use of seekg() and tellg() functions
Proper error handling
Close file after operations

questions.question: Write a menu-driven C++ program to perform file operations: create file, append data, read and display, and exit. Use proper file opening modes for each operation.
keyPoints: Display menu options
Case 1: Create/Write - use ofstream with ios::out
Case 2: Append - use ofstream with ios::app
Case 3: Read - use ifstream with ios::in
Case 4: Exit - break from loop
Include error handling for file operations
Close files after each operation
Loop until user exits
